dimensions.note: Docs-only change (README workflow example and audit-gate.sh helper) plus a manifest version bump. No credentials introduced; the corrected example still passes AWS auth via profile/env, and the account ID is a caller-supplied workflow input (placeholder AWS_ACCOUNT_ID), never a secret.
No report source (s3_bucket_audit.ts) changed; existing structured logging is untouched.

No control flow changed in the extension. The documented audit-gate.sh keeps its exit-code contract (0 no-trip / 1 tripped / 2 unreadable); a missing gate field still routes to exit 2 rather than silently passing, now that it reads the sanitized data name (report-jentz-aws-s3-bucket-audit-json) and the .content-nested gate fields.

No code change; existing unit tests still pass under deno test (133 passed). The corrected workflow example was validated end-to-end with a throwaway workflow (swamp workflow validate: all checks passed) and then deleted. The audit-gate.sh data name and .content envelope were corrected to match empirically verified persisted-artifact behavior.
Report-only extension; it renders collected workflow data and creates/updates/deletes no external resources.
No external API client in this extension; it consumes swamp step data/artifacts only.
No new files, handles, or network clients; the manifest additionalFiles set is unchanged (README.md, LICENSE.md).

Reviewed the edited README: the workflow example and audit-gate.sh use placeholders only (bucket name my-iac-state-bucket, account ID passed as the AWS_ACCOUNT_ID input placeholder). No real account IDs, IPs, hostnames, ARNs, or CIDR blocks. Manifest version bumped to 2026.07.19.0.
